name: code_run
description: Execute code — python scripts or bash/shell commands. Use 'type' to specify language (python/bash). Use 'timeout' for max execution time. Prefer python for complex operations; use bash only for system commands.

name: file_read
description: Read file content. Supports start line, keyword search, and line count limits. Use keyword for searching within files.

name: file_write
description: Write content to a file. Use mode='append' to add to existing file, mode='overwrite' to replace. For small edits, prefer file_patch.

name: file_patch
description: Patch a file by replacing a unique block of old_content with new_content. For small, precise edits. Use file_read first to verify current content.

old_content.description: The exact text block to find (must be unique in the file)

name: web_scan
description: Scan web page content. For simple web tasks, use code_run with requests/beautifulsoup4 instead. Full browser integration requires setup.

name: web_execute_js
description: Execute JavaScript in browser context. Requires browser integration. For web automation, use code_run with selenium/playwright instead.

save_to_file.description: Save JS return value to this file path

name: ask_user
description: Ask the user a question and wait for their response. Use for clarifications, confirmations, and decisions on irreversible operations.

name: update_working_checkpoint
description: Update working memory — set key info and related SOP for the current task. Call at task start or mid-task to focus the agent's attention.

key_info.description: Key information to remember for this task

name: start_long_term_update
description: Distill task experience into long-term memory. Call after completing a complex task to save proven knowledge. Only store verified, durable facts and workflows.

summary.description: Brief summary of what was learned

name: wiki_ingest
description: Ingest knowledge into the wiki — creates a new page or merges into existing one (append strategy, never replaces). Stored in global scope by default, or in the current project scope if a project is selected. This is the compounding mechanism: knowledge grows across sessions. Use cross-references like [[page-name]] to link pages.

content.description: Markdown content for the wiki page

name: wiki_query
description: Search wiki pages by keywords, tags, and category. Returns matching pages with relevance snippets. Searches global scope and current project scope.

query.description: Search text (matched against title, tags, and content)

name: fact_add
description: Add a verified fact to the knowledge base. Stored in global scope by default, or in the current project scope if a project is selected. Facts have trust scores that evolve with use: helpful facts gain trust (+0.05), unhelpful facts lose trust (-0.10). Use for quick, structured knowledge.

name: fact_search
description: Search facts by keywords with trust-based ranking. Searches global scope and current project scope.

name: fact_feedback
description: Mark a fact as helpful or unhelpful. Only works on facts you accessed this session via fact_search or db_query. Helpful feedback bumps trust (+0.05), unhelpful drops trust (-0.10). Unhelpful requires a reason (min 10 chars). Feedback events are recorded for evolution loss integration.

id.description: The fact ID to provide feedback on (must be from fact_search/db_query results this session)

helpful.description: Whether the fact was helpful (true) or unhelpful (false)

reason.description: Why the fact was helpful/unhelpful. Required (min 10 chars) when helpful=false, optional when helpful=true.

name: db_query
description: Execute SQL queries against your knowledge database. You are the DBA of your own memory — write SELECT to retrieve knowledge, INSERT to add structured data, UPDATE to modify records. For precise, relational queries this replaces wiki_query/fact_search. Example: SELECT f.content, f.trust_score FROM facts f WHERE f.category='environment' AND f.trust_score > 0.7. Sandbox rules: only SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE allowed, no DROP/DELETE/ALTER. Max 50 rows per query. Use db_schema first to understand available tables and columns.

sql.description: SQL query to execute (SELECT, INSERT, or UPDATE only)

name: db_schema
description: Inspect your knowledge database schema — see available tables, columns, types, and row counts. Use this to understand what you can query before writing SQL with db_query.

name: wiki_export
description: Export wiki pages to markdown files for human browsing and git tracking. Creates .nova/wiki/*.md files with YAML frontmatter. Does not modify the database — markdown is a read-only view.

name: cron
description: Manage scheduled cron jobs. Create recurring or one-shot tasks that run automatically. Use action='create' to schedule a task, action='list' to see all jobs, action='remove' to delete a job, action='run' to trigger a job immediately. Jobs run in fresh sessions — prompts must be self-contained. You can schedule your own maintenance, reviews, or experiments.

prompt.description: For create: self-contained prompt to execute when the job runs

schedule.description: For create: schedule type — '30m' (once in 30min), 'every 2h' (recurring interval), '0 9 * * *' (cron expression)

name.description: Optional friendly name for the job

job_id.description: For remove/run: the job ID to target

name: skill_add
description: Crystallize a repeatable workflow into a skill. Stored in global scope by default, or in the current project scope if a project is selected. Skills are structured SOPs that improve over time through usage tracking and version evolution. Write skills when: (1) you completed a multi-step task that you'd repeat, (2) you discovered a workflow pattern worth preserving, (3) you learned pitfalls that prevent common mistakes. Format: numbered imperative steps, triggers for when to activate, pitfalls for what to avoid. Last step should always be a verification/check step.

name.description: Skill name (slug-style, e.g. 'deploy-flask-app'). Must be unique.

description.description: One-line summary of what this skill accomplishes

triggers.description: Comma-separated keywords that indicate when to apply this skill (e.g. 'deploy,flask,ssh'). These are used for proactive skill matching — when a task contains these keywords, the skill is suggested automatically.

steps.description: Ordered list of imperative steps. Number each step. Last step should verify success. Example: '1. Read config file', '2. Validate schema', '3. Verify: check output matches expected'

pitfalls.description: Common mistakes and anti-patterns to avoid when executing this skill

name: skill_feedback
description: Mark a skill as helpful or unhelpful. Only works on skills you accessed this session via skill_search. Helpful feedback increases success rate, unhelpful decreases it. Unhelpful requires a reason (min 10 chars). Feedback events are recorded for evolution loss integration.

name.description: The skill name to provide feedback on (must be from skill_search results this session)

helpful.description: Whether the skill was helpful (true) or unhelpful (false)

reason.description: Why the skill was helpful/unhelpful. Required (min 10 chars) when helpful=false, optional when helpful=true.

name: skill_search
description: Search for crystallized skills/SOPs by keywords. Returns skills with steps, triggers, pitfalls, and success rates. Use before starting complex tasks to leverage proven workflows. Skills improve over time — high success_rate skills are battle-tested. Searches global scope and current project scope.

query.description: Search keywords — matches against name, description, triggers, tags

name: link_add
description: Create an explicit relationship between two knowledge items. Links enable cascade feedback — marking a fact unhelpful flags linked skills for review. Use link types: depends_on (skill needs this fact), related_to (connected topics), derived_from (knowledge came from this source), contradicts (conflicting knowledge).

source_type.description: Type of the source knowledge item

source_name.description: Name of the source item (for skills/wiki)

target_type.description: Type of the target knowledge item

target_name.description: Name of the target item (for skills/wiki)

name: link_search
description: Search knowledge links by optional filters. Find dependencies, related items, and connections between facts, skills, and wiki pages. Use to discover what knowledge items depend on each other.

name: cluster_search
description: Search for composed knowledge bundles — facts, skills, and wiki pages grouped by topic tag. Returns structured bundles with relevance scores based on tag overlap. Use when you need a comprehensive knowledge package for a task type. Searches global scope and current project scope.

query.description: Search keywords — matched against tags of facts, skills, and wiki pages

name: project_create
description: Create a new project scope for knowledge isolation. Projects allow you to compartmentalize knowledge — facts, skills, and wiki pages scoped to a project are only visible when that project is selected. Use project_select to switch scope.

name: project_select
description: Select a project scope for subsequent knowledge operations. When a project is selected, reads include global + project-scoped knowledge, and writes default to project scope. Pass project_id=null to switch back to global scope.

project_id.description: Project ID to select, or null to switch to global scope

name: project_list
description: List all projects with their IDs, names, descriptions, and creation dates.

name: project_info
description: Get detailed info about a project including scoped knowledge counts (facts, skills, wiki pages).

name: fact_promote
description: Promote a project-scoped fact to global scope, making it accessible across all projects.

id.description: The fact ID to promote to global scope

name: skill_promote
description: Promote a project-scoped skill to global scope, making it accessible across all projects.

name.description: The skill name to promote to global scope

name: wiki_promote
description: Promote a project-scoped wiki page to global scope, making it accessible across all projects.

slug.description: The wiki page slug to promote to global scope

name: project_learn
description: Learn about the current project directory — scan it and return structured info for knowledge generation. Reads README, CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md, package configs, workspace configs, infrastructure configs, and file tree. The agent then uses this scan data to create facts, wiki pages, skills, and knowledge links about the project's architecture, conventions, and patterns.

project_root.description: Path to project root directory. Defaults to cwd.

depth.description: Scan depth: quick=top-level files only, standard=top-level + src structure, deep=full analysis with nested directories
